PLAINTIFF'S ALTERNATIVE RESPONSE TO MOTION/ TO REQUEST PERMISSION TO SERVE DEFENDANT'S LAWYER FILED BY PLAINTIFF SZABO, TIBOR ATTILA
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-23-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CARLENE LAUGHLIN'S MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED. "[A] DEFENDANT HAS AN ABSOLUTE RIGHT TO DEMAND THAT PROCESS BE ISSUED AGAINST HIM IN A MANNER PRESCRIBED BY LAW. AS A GENERAL RULE, HOWEVER, THE PROVISIONS OF THE RULES GOVERNING SERVICE OF PROCESS ARE TO BE LIBERALLY CONSTRUED." (MANNESMANN DEMAG, LTD. V. SUP. CRT. (1985) 172 CAL.APP.3D 1118, 1123.) PLAINTIFF TIBOR SZABO FAILS TO SHOW THAT HE SERVED MS. LAUGHLIN IN A MANNER IN SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE. THE PROOF OF SERVICE FILED ON MAY 29, 2018 DOES NOT CLEARLY EXPLAIN THE MODE OF SERVICE AND THERE IS INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE SHOWING PERSONAL OR SUBSTITUTE SERVICE. MR. SZABO'S REQUEST TO SERVE MS. LAUGHLIN'S COUNSEL IS DENIED. THERE IS NO AUTHORITY THAT WOULD ALLOW SUCH AN ORDER UNDER THE PRESENT CIRCUMSTANCES. APPLICATIONS TO SERVE BY PUBLICATION ARE TO BE MADE IN DEPARTMENT 206 PER SF LOCAL RULE 3.1C(2). MR. SZABO MUST MAKE A SEPARATE MOTION THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1324 TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TIBOR SZABO'S MOTION TO REQUEST PERMISSION TO AMEND THE CLAIM THE 2ND TIME IS OFF CALENDAR. THE UNTIMELY MAIL SERVICE ON AUGUST 30, 2018 DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CODE OF CIV. PROC. 1005. THE COURT WILL REVIEW THE PROOF OF SERVICE AND IF THERE WAS A MISTAKE, THE COURT WILL SEND NOTICE TO THE PLAINTIFF TO CORRECT ITS ERROR. JUDGE: ANDREW Y.S. CHENG; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/AC

Off Calendar Aug-08-2019 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance, the Court ordered the case dismissed pursuant to Government Code Section 68608(b). The court finds that a less severe sanction would not be effective in light of the history of failure to comply with the court's orders and rules. All previously imposed sanctions against the plaintiff are vacated. Judge: GARRETT L. WONG; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Proceedings not reported. (610)

Motion To Request Permission To Amend The Claim The 2nd Time
Off Calendar Sep-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tibor Szabo, Plaintiff appearing in pro per;
No other appearances on the matter.


RULING - The matter was discussed. The court ruled as follows: PLAINTIFF TIBOR SZABO's Motion To Request Permission To Amend The Claim The 2nd Time is off calendar. The untimely mail service on August 30, 2018 does not comply with Code of Civ. Proc. 1005. The court will review the proof of service and if there was a mistake, the court will send notice to the Plaintiff to correct its error. Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/ac

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash
Off Calendar Jul-23-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tibor Atilla Szabo, Plaintiff appearing in pro per;
Atty. Min K. Kang of Coddington Hicks & Danforth, Tel. No. 1-310-981-4920
appearing for the Defendant by telephone appearance.

RULING- The matter was discussed. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Carlene Laughlin's motion to quash is granted. "[A] defendant has an absolute right to demand that process be issued against him in a manner prescribed by law. As a general rule, however, the provisions of the rules governing service of process are to be liberally construed." (Mannesmann DeMag, Ltd. v. Sup. Crt. (1985) 172 Cal.App.3d 1118, 1123.) Plaintiff Tibor Szabo fails to show that he served Ms. Laughlin in a manner in substantial compliance with the requirements of the Code of Civil Procedure. The proof of service filed on May 29, 2018 does not clearly explain the mode of service and there is insufficient evidence showing personal or substitute service. Mr. Szabo's request to serve Ms. Laughlin's counsel is denied. There is no authority that would allow such an order under the present circumstances. Applications to serve by publication are to be made in department 206 per SF Local Rule 3.1C(2). Mr. Szabo must make a separate motion that complies with CRC 3.1324 to amend the complaint. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
